Krause (CEO, ex-Army Research Lab neuromorphic semiconductor researcher, ex-AlleyCorp deep-tech investor, US Army National Guard vet), Jorge Colindres (President, ex-AlleyCorp), Prof. Gerbrand Ceder (CSO, UC Berkeley materials science professor, ex-MIT R.P. methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $55M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Radical AI is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $55M to date. The founding team combines deep technical expertise—CEO Joseph Krause from the Army Research Lab with neuromorphic semiconductor experience, and Chief Science Officer Prof. Gerbrand Ceder, a UC Berkeley materials scientist—alongside operational leadership from AlleyCorp. The relevant peer universe comprises nine private robotics companies (including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, and Exotec) and one public comparable.

Given the nascent nature of Radical AI's stage and the heterogeneity of the robotics sector, a robust peer-median approach proved inapplicable; filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier peers in Robotics & Manufacturing yielded insufficient datapoints (fewer than three). Instead, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Applied to the $55M in total raised as a proxy for current value, a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement implies a valuation of $138M. Sensitivity analysis around this multiplier—applying 2.15x for the low case and 2.875x for the high case—establishes a range of $117M to $158M.

The analysis supports a median valuation of $138M with a spread of approximately $41M around the center. Key uncertainties include team execution risk in a capital-intensive sector, the timing and scale of next institutional funding, and validation of core technical claims in neuromorphic manufacturing systems. The single-method foundation warrants reassessment upon availability of closer-stage comparable data.
